title: Extraordinary Animals
subTitle: This wildlife documentary follows Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of experts as they travel across seas to find the world's most extraordinary animal stories. The series focuses on animal intelligence, exploring remarkable behaviours, relationships and actions of various species. In Florida, Hugh discovers the unusual relationship between the predatory alligator and the placid manatee, while zoologist Lucy Cooke travels to Canada to reveal how raccoons are cleverly adapting to city life. Biologist Patrick Aryee is in Cambodia to explore why the Sun Bear is one of the world's shrewdest animals and conservationist Giles Clark discusses elephants and emotional intelligence while in Kenya.

title: How Deadly
subTitle: Ann Jones examines crocodile scenes in films, including Mick Dundee's crocodile fight, the crocodile that Betty White feeds in Lake Placid, and the man-eating crocodile in Rogue.

title: Gardening Australia
subTitle: This programme provides practical, realistic, and credible horticultural and gardening advice, inspiring and entertaining all Australian gardeners around the nation.

title: Landline
subTitle: A programme bringing news from rural Australia to the nation. The multi-award winning show typically features stories, ranging across agri-politics and economics, business and product innovation, animal and crop science, and regional infrastructure.

title: Planet America
subTitle: Examines America's complex and controversial political news, views and developments, all with a satirical edge.

title: ABC News With Joe O'Brien
subTitle: The host presents information about the social, economic and political issues of the day and provides updates on current events.

subTitle: Chef John Ralley and Paralympic swimmer Ellie Cole join Adam to make some fast fuel.
